key_insights.insight: Foot pain significantly impacts physical, emotional, and mental health, hindering basic activities and overall well-being.
key_insights.quote: So it really starts to deter your physical health, your emotional health, your mental health.

key_insights.insight: Footwear can significantly affect foot function, with narrow shoes altering foot structure and function.
key_insights.quote: If you don't use it, you're going to lose it.And so that's why I think footwear has such a big implication on our function.

key_insights.insight: Walking is an underrated and underutilized activity; even small increases in daily steps can significantly reduce mortality risk.
key_insights.quote: If you were to walk an additional 500 steps in a day, your baseline is 2,500, you canreduce your risk of cardiovascular mortality by 7%.

key_insights.insight: Orthotics can be beneficial initially for offloading pain, but should be used in conjunction with foot strengthening exercises for a long-term solution.
key_insights.quote: It's where this insert and strengthen your footbecause the goal should be to have an exit strategyfor the insert and get your foot back on the ground.

key_insights.insight: The foot's condition can serve as a window to overall body mechanics, revealing issues beyond just the foot itself.
key_insights.quote: So when we start to see things happen at the foot, it's a window. It's a window to what's going on,not only at the foot, but everywhere else in the kinetic chain.

key_insights.insight: Cushioned shoes can reduce sensory input from the ground, affecting balance and proprioception.
key_insights.quote: The problem with cushion is that the more stuff that's between your foot and the ground,the less you feel. So there's a loss of sensory acuity.There's a loss of sensory perception.

key_insights.insight: Overstriding when running is detrimental, as it inhibits proper shock absorption.
key_insights.quote: I want my foot to strike as close to my center of mass as possible.

key_insights.insight: Toe spring in running shoes can weaken the intrinsic muscles of the foot if used consistently.
key_insights.quote: So there is research that shows when you put your footin a position with toe spring,you will weaken the intrinsic muscles of the foot.

actionable_advice.advice: Transition to shoes with a wide toe box to allow toes to splay naturally.
actionable_advice.how_to: Gradually increase the time spent wearing wide toe box shoes, starting with short periods and progressing as comfort allows.

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate micro walks (5-10 minutes) throughout the day to increase daily step count.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set reminders to take short walking breaks during work or other sedentary activities. A five-minute walk is about 500 steps, a 10-minute walk is about a thousand steps.

actionable_advice.advice: Perform regular foot exercises using tools like toe spacers, resistance bands, and massage balls.
actionable_advice.how_to: Use a foot health kit that contains toe spacers, strengtheners, bands, and a ball to engage muscles in the foot

actionable_advice.advice: Work on ankle mobility and calf strength to support lower leg function and prevent injuries.
actionable_advice.how_to: Perform static and dynamic stretching exercises for the ankle. Train the soleus by doing seated single-leg calf raises with appropriate weight.

actionable_advice.advice: Pay attention to gait and avoid overstriding when running.
actionable_advice.how_to: Focus on landing with the foot closer to the center of mass, reducing the impact of each stride.

memorable_stories: A 27-year-old patient diagnosed with heel pain was told to limit his steps to 2,500 per day, leading to depression. By gradually increasing his step count with micro-walks and addressing his footwear, he was able to improve his physical and emotional well-being.
The guest observed barefoot construction workers in Belize with wide, strong feet, illustrating the foot's natural design and strength without constrictive footwear.
Guest's daughter using bands around her fingers for rock climbing inspired a design for resistance bands around toes to strengthen the feet
contrarian_claims: Hiking boots marketed for ankle support may restrict ankle dorsiflexion, transferring load to the knee.
Cushioned shoes decrease sensory perception and negatively impact proprioception.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Your Toes Can Predict If You'll Die Early And Heres's How To Fix Your Plantar Fasciitis! - Dr. Courtney Conley (Foot Specialist)
